overview: The YAML Filter extracts translatable content from [YAML files](http://www.yaml.org/) and supports [Ruby on Rails](http://rubyonrails.org/) message variables. It extracts string values from key-value pairs, maps, and arrays, assigning each entry a hierarchical name built from the sequence of all parent identifiers (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). The filter handles Unicode BOM detection for encoding and preserves original line-break types in output.
limitations: Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document â€” all text will appear on a single line.

If an illegal character is introduced during translation, the user must manually add double or single quotes to keep the target YAML valid. The filter issues a **WARNING** when it can detect this scenario.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: If the file has a Unicode BOM (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.), that encoding is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified when opening the document applies.
**Line-breaks**: Output line-break type matches the original input document.
**Identifiers**: Each extracted entry's name is the `/`-separated path of all parent keys, e.g. `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`.

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls the default extraction behavior for key-value pairs.

- **Enabled**: All key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those whose key matches the `excludeKeys` regex.
- **Disabled**: No key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those whose key matches the `excludeKeys` regex (inverted behavior).

The `excludeKeys` pattern always acts as the **inverse** of whichever mode is selected here.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help:
Extracts string values that are not directly associated with a key, such as bare strings in arrays:

```yaml
list: [one, two, three]
```

Without this option, only key-value pairs like `key: value` are extracted.

extraction.excludeKeys.help:
A regular expression matched against keys. Its behavior **depends on the Extract All setting**:

- When **Extract All** is enabled: keys matching this pattern are **excluded** from extraction.
- When **Extract All** is disabled: keys matching this pattern are **included** for extraction.

When **Use Full Path** is also enabled, the regex is matched against the full key path (e.g., `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`) rather than just the immediate key.
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Java regular expression pattern matched against YAML keys (or full key paths when `keys.useFullPath` is enabled).
examples: `^config/.*` â€” exclude all keys under the `config` parent

dependsOn.condition: Inverts behavior: when extractAll is true, matching keys are excluded; when false, matching keys are included
When enabled, regex is matched against the full `/`-separated key path instead of just the immediate key

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the YAML key as the `resname` (resource name) of the extracted text unit. This provides a stable, human-readable identifier for each translatable entry â€” equivalent to `resname` in XLIFF.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the complete `/`-separated path of nested keys as the name instead of just the immediate key.

For example, given nested YAML:
```yaml
menu:
  value:
    popup:
      label: "Click me"
```
The name becomes `menu/value/popup/label` instead of just `label`.

When enabled, the `excludeKeys` regex also matches against the full path.

dependsOn.condition: Must be enabled for this option to take effect

output.escapeNonAscii.help: Escapes characters outside the ASCII range in the output using YAML unicode escape sequences. Useful when the target system or workflow requires ASCII-safe files.

output.wrap.help: Controls whether long lines are wrapped in the output. Note that regardless of this setting, wrapped lines from the **original** source are always unwrapped during processing â€” this is a known limitation of the filter.
notes: Wrapped lines in the original source will always be unwrapped in the output, regardless of this setting.

output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.help: When a sub-filter is configured (e.g., `okf_html`), this option causes YAML literal block scalars (`|`) to also be processed through the sub-filter. Without this, only regular string values are sub-filtered.

inlineCodes.help:
Defines regex patterns to detect inline codes within extracted text. Any match is converted to an inline code placeholder in the extracted content.

**Cannot be used together with sub-filtering** â€” if a sub-filter is specified, inline code patterns are ignored.

The default pattern matches:
- `printf`-style format specifiers (e.g., `%d`, `%s`, `%2$f`)
- Escape sequences (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, etc.)
- Numbered placeholders (`{0}`, `{1}`, etc.)
notes: **Warning:** This option cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option.

examples.title: Ruby on Rails i18n file
examples.description: A typical Rails locale file showing extractable content. The filter extracts all highlighted string values and assigns hierarchical names based on the key path.
examples.input:
```yaml
fr:
  activerecord:
    errors:
      template:
        header:
          list: [one, two, three]
          map: {key: value, key2: value2}
          one: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: 1 erreur"
          other: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: {{count}} erreurs."
        body: "Veuillez vĂ©rifier les champs suivants :"
      messages:
        inclusion: "n'est pas inclus(e) dans la liste"
        exclusion: "n'est pas disponible"
```

examples.title: Selective extraction with key exclusion
examples.description: Extract all pairs except metadata keys by using `extractAll: true` with an `excludeKeys` regex pattern.
